California
Fair POlitical
Practices Commission
January 2, 1992
Ronald L. Thonpson
Executive Director
orange Redevelopnent Agency
Robert o. Franks
General. counsel
orange Redevelopment Agency
City of Orange
orange civic center
300 E. Chapman Avenue
orange, cA 92666-L591
Re: Your Request for Advice
our File No. A-91-509
Dear Mr. Thompson and Mr' Franks:
You have requested advice on behalf of the city of orange
(the 'lcity") and the Orange Redevelopment Agency (the rragencyrr)
regarding the conf l ict-of- interest provisions of the PoIitical
Reiorm ait (ttre ttrc;rri .1
Please note that our advice only pertains to !.!IEgIg conduct
and decisionsi we do not evaluate any conduct that has already
occurred. In addition, our advi.ce is linited only to provisions
of the Act. we cannot provide advice about other confl'ict-of-
interest laws which rnay apply to your situation, such as
Government code Section 109o and the doctrine of inconpatible
offices or activities. 
Please contact the Attorney General's
office for advice with respect to these areas of the law.
OUESTION
May the principal special counsel participate in the agency's
decision to hire bond counsel?
'I' 
covernment code Sections 81OOO-91015. AII statutory references
are to the Governnent code unless other$rise indicated. 
Cornmission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations. Sectj'on
18ooo, et seq. A11 references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 5 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 8OO . P.O. Box 807 o Sacramento CA 958O4-O8O7 o (916) 322.5660


Our File No. A-9■-509
Page 2
CONCLI'A ION
The principal special counsel may not participate in or
attempt to influence a general agency decision concerning whether
to hire bond counsel if there wiII be a materi'a} financial effect
on his 1aw f irrn. Under Regulation 18702.1(a) (1), he may not
participate in or attempt to influence a specific aqency decision
to hire his firn as bond counsel.
FACTA
The city of orange has established the orange Redevelopment
Agency in order to plan and implernent various redevelopment
projects in the city. 
The agency currently contracts with a
particular law firn (the 'tfirnrr) to serve as special counsel to
the agency. one of the partners in the firm has principal
responsibility within the f irrn to render leqaI services to the
agency. That attorney (the "principal special counselr') is
conpensated only by the firn.
Customarj.ty, the agency has contracted with the firm to serve
as bond counsel. At the present time, the agency has issued a
reguest for proposals ('rRFPt') for bond counsel for an upcoming
bond issue. The agency has distributed the RFP to a staff-
prepared list of twelve law firms, including the f irrn. It is your
undlrstanding that the firn intends to answer the RFP and submit a
proposal to act as bond counsel for the agency, either pursuant to
Lhe- terms of the existing contract or pursuant to an amended or
new contract.
The decision to issue bonds r.ras nade without consultation
with, the rendering of advice, or any other form of participation
by the principal special counsel. Neither the firn nor the
piinciplt 
special counsel. will participate in or otherwise attempt
to influenci the criteria, 
the evaluation or the selection of the
bond counsel.
The principal special. counsel is a partner in the firm, with
a less than 1oZ ownershj.p interest in the f irrn. Any decisions
nade pursuant to the existing agency/firm contract for attorney
services will not affect other firn clients.
ANAI,YSI S
On February 8, 1991 we issued Advice Letter I-90-686 to
Mr. Luis Rodriquez, who also requested advice on behalf of the
city and the agency. That letter concluded that the prj.ncipal
special counsel is a public official 
under the Act. The letter
also evaluated the confl ict-of- interest provisions that pertain
\.rhen the principal special counsel's f irrn contracts with the


Our Flle No. A-9■
-509
Page 3
agency to serve as bond counsel. l.tuch of the advice rendered in
the Rodriquez letter also pertains to your current questions,
which are:
1. 
t'May the agency contract lrith the firm for bond counsel
services if the principal special counsel does not make,
participate in the making or otherwise attenpt to infLuence the
agency's selection of the f irn?ti
2. 
'tMay the agency ask the firm to serve as bond counsel
within the paraneters of the existing agency/firm contract for
attorney services? "
The questions you have posed are not within the jurisdiction
of the Act. The conf l ict-of- interest provisions of the Act only
provide guidance with respect to whether a public official 
may
participate in governmental decisions; it does not detennine the
iuirity- of an agency2 to make decisions or contracts. Therefore,
we have rephrased your question within the Paraneters of the Act
and answered accordingly.
The Act prohibits a public official 
frorn naking,
participating in, or using his official position to infLuence a
governnental decision in which he knows or has reason to know he
has a financial interest. 
(section 87100.) A public official 
has
a financial. interest in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable
that the decision vilI 
have a rnaterial financlal effect,
distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on the
official 
or a nenber of his imrnediate farnily or on, among other
things:
(a) Any business entity in which the public
official 
has a direct or indirect investnent $/orth
one thousand dollars ($1000) or nore.
(b) Any real property in which the public
official 
has a direct or indirect interest $rorth
one thousand dollars (S1000) or more.
(c) Any source of incorne, other than gifts
and other than loans by a connercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terrns available to the public uithout regard to
official 
status, aggregating two hundred fifty
doLlars ($250) or more in value provided to,
received by or prornised to the public official
within 12 months prior to the tine when the
decision is nade.
1' 
However, a public official of the agency lrho has a conflict of
interest under the Act, nay not participate in or attenpt to
influence any agency decision regarding the particular natter in
which he is disqualified.


Our File NO. A-9■
-509
Page 4
(d) Any business entity in which the public
offj.cial is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
enployee, or holds any position of nanagement.
Section 87103 (a) - (d) .
Since the prj,ncipal sPecial counsel is a partner in the firm,
he is required to disqualify hirnself fron participating in any
governnental decision which will foreseeably and rnaterially affect
the f irn.
Foreseeabi I itv
The effect of a decision is reasonably foreseeable if there
is a substantial likelihood that it wil-l- occur. certainty is not
required; however, an effect that is nerely a possibility is not
reisonabiy foreseeable. (Downev cares v. Downev communitv
Develoomeit com. (1987) 196 cal.App.3d 983; In re Thorner (1975) 1
FPPC Ops. 198. )
Materialitv
The comnission has adopted several regulations which define
material financial effect. 
Regulation 18702 sets forth the
generaL guidelines for determining $/hether an official's 
financial
interest-in a decision is trnaterialrr as reguired by section 87103'
If the officiat's 
financial interest is directtv involved in the
decision, Regulation L8702.1 apPlies to deterrnine materiality' 
If
the officialis 
financial interest is indirectlv affected by the
decision, Regulations L8702.2 through ]-8702.6 apply to deternine
whether the effect of the decision is naterial.
The question regarding the principal special counsel's
participa€ion has both a broad and narro!, application. 
If the
|eneral- question concerns his participation in an agency decision
io hire Eond counsel, his firn will be indirectLy affected by the
decision and Regulation 18702.2 applies. This regulation, which
defines material. financial effect on a business entity indirectly
involved in a decisj.on, is very fact specific and is based on
dollar threshold amounts of net tangible assets, pre-tax income,
gross revenues and expenses. Since s/e do not have any financial
inf ormatj.on about the firm, we do not know which subdivision of
Regulation :-8702.2 applies to this situation. 
Therefore, it is
incumbent upon you to analyze this regulation accordingJ,y.
If the specific question concerns the principal specia)-
counsel's participation in an agency decision to hire his firm as
bond counsel, Regulation 18702.1 applies. This regulation, which


Our File No. A-9■-509
Page 5
SH:」RS:aa 
―
s incere 1y ,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting ceneral counsel
defines material financial effect when an official's 
economic
interest is directly involved in a decision, was discussed in the
Rodriquez Letter. 
In sumnary, under 18702.1(a)(1), the effect of
a decision is deemed material if a source of incorne of S25o or
nore in the preceding 12 months is directlY inYolved in,a decision
before the oificial's 
agency. since the firm is ansvrering the RFP
and is one of the candidates for bond counsel, they are directly
involved in the agency's decision. Therefore, the principal
special counsel nay not participate in or attempt to influence the
dlcision of the agency regarding the selection of bond counsel'
We realize that this letter has not answered your specific
questions, which are not under our jurisdiction, 
but hope that we
liurr" p.orided necessary guidance. If you have further questions,
please contact ne at (916) 322-59OL-
By:  ::造

Stecher
Legal Division
